text:
Once upon a time in the beautiful forest where I lived, there was a peculiar little fox who loved nothing more than collecting shiny objects. You could spot him anywhere—his golden collar gleamed even when he was nowhere around.

Every day he would go on adventures to collect shiny things like shiny beetles or shiny stones. He loved shiny stones so much that he would even collect tiny gleaming chips of wood.

One day the tiny chip decided to chip away at a particularly big oak tree. The oak tree was ancient and wise. It watched the chip wander through countless rooms filled with treasures.

One day while the chip was away gathering shiny things nestled deep in an ant nest, a wise old owl discovered him.

"Ah," the owl said. "You have beautiful shiny things." Every creature in the forest wanted the shiny things.

The chip said, "That's just a silly chip!" But it couldn Chip wanted desperately.
